channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kristin Cabot Shares Details About Her Viral Coldplay Kiss and Estranged Husband's Presence
item.description: Kristin Cabot, who gained international attention from a viral kiss cam moment at a Coldplay concert in 2025, opened up about the event in an interview with Oprah Winfrey on 'The Oprah Podcast.' Cabot...

item.title: National Youth Service Begins Countrywide Recruitment to Empower Young Kenyans
item.description: The National Youth Service (NYS) has kicked off a countrywide recruitment exercise, inviting young Kenyans aged between 18 and 24 years to enlist and serve the nation while gaining vital skills and di...

item.title: Pakistan Backs Three-Month Extension of UNAMA Mandate, Highlights Surge in Cross-Border Terrorism from Afghanistan
item.description: The United Nations Security Council has unanimously approved a three-month extension for the United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan (UNAMA), with Pakistan among the 15 member states voting i...

item.title: Alexandra Paul arrested during animal rights protest in Wisconsin
item.description: Alexandra Paul, best known for her role as Stephanie Holden on Baywatch, was arrested in Wisconsin over the weekend after participating in an animal rights protest at Ridglan Farms, a facility that br...

item.title: Residents evacuate as fire damages Tuggeranong apartment roof
item.description: On Tuesday afternoon, a fire broke out on the second-floor balcony of an apartment on Athllon Drive in Greenway, Tuggeranong, prompting the swift response of ACT Fire and Rescue. Station Officer Jake ...

item.title: South Africa Prepares for Sixth Investment Conference Amid Positive Economic Growth
item.description: South Africa is gearing up for its sixth Investment Conference, set to take place at the Sandton Convention Centre on 31 March 2026. Minister of Trade, Industry and Competition Parks Tau highlighted t...

item.title: RBA increases official cash rate to 4.1% amid inflation and geopolitical concerns
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has raised the official cash rate for the second month in a row, moving it up by 0.25 percentage points to 4.1%, from 3.85%. The decision came after a narrowly spli...

item.title: Practical Steps Canadians Can Take to Reduce Fraud Risk
item.description: Canadians are increasingly targeted by fraud across email, phone, online, and text channels, with nearly half reporting attempts in the past three months. Despite the prevalence, very few cases are of...

item.title: 537 Young People Graduate as Certified Real Estate Practitioners in KwaZulu-Natal Through Services SETA Programme
item.description: A significant step towards transforming South Africa's real estate sector took place on Saturday at the Durban International Convention Centre, where 537 young people graduated as certified real estat...

item.title: Safari Rally sees renewed interest among local fans
item.description: The Safari Rally is steadily becoming a favourite event among Kenyans, reflecting a revival of interest in the iconic motorsport. David Githae notes that the rally should consider expanding its route ...

item.title: Vincent Massey Trojans secure AAAA provincial hockey title with 4-1 victory over Oak Park
item.description: The Vincent Massey Trojans have reclaimed their title as Manitoba champions in boys high school hockey, winning the Provincial AAAA Boys High School Hockey Championship with a 4-1 triumph over the Oak...

item.title: Seven Medical Colleges Penalised for Non-Disclosure of Intern and Resident Stipends
item.description: The National Medical Commission (NMC) has taken stern action against seven medical colleges across India for not publicly disclosing the stipends paid to MBBS interns and postgraduate medical resident...

item.title: Marikina City encourages government employees to bike to work amid fuel crisis
item.description: In response to rising fuel prices, the Marikina local government has introduced an initiative called “Padyak Papasok: Bike to Work.” Launched by Mayor Marjorie Ann Teodoro on March 16, 2026, the progr...

item.title: Hilda Baci and Miskay Launch Capsule Fashion Collection Reflecting Her Personal Style
item.description: Hilda Baci don extend her talents from kitchen go fashion world with her new capsule collection wey she drop in partnership with Miskay Boutique. Dem call am 'Signature' collection, and e dey reflect ...

item.title: Kenya to Export Agricultural Products to China Without Tariffs Starting May 2026
item.description: Kenya is set to start exporting a range of agricultural products to China duty-free from May 1, 2026, Agriculture CS Mutahi Kagwe has confirmed. This move comes after President William Ruto's recent s...

item.title: India’s Strategic Balancing in West Asia Amid Rising Regional Tensions
item.description: The recent sinking of the Iranian warship IRIS Dena by the United States in the Indian Ocean on March 4, 2026, has brought the West Asia conflict uncomfortably close to South Asia. Millions of South A...

item.title: Government ensures salaries of employees released ahead of Eid
item.description: ISLAMABAD – Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has directed that salaries of all government employees be released before Eid, assuring the public that his government is working on both immediate economic r...

item.title: Punjab Approves Purchase of 10 Vehicles for High Court Judges Amid Austerity Measures
item.description: LAHORE: The Punjab cabinet on Monday gave its approval for buying 10 new vehicles for the superior judiciary, while simultaneously announcing contributions to the Prime Minister’s austerity fund. Init...

item.title: Gregory Bovino to Retire After Career in U.S. Border Patrol
item.description: Gregory Bovino, a long-serving U.S. Border Patrol official, announced his plans to retire at the end of March 2026. Bovino joined the Border Patrol in 1996 and held prominent roles, including commande...

item.title: Israel reports destruction of plane previously used by Iran's late Supreme Leader
item.description: On Monday, March 16, 2026, Israeli military announced say dem don destroy one aircraft wey the late Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei use before, and e dey at Mehrabad airport for Tehran. The plane ...
